title: Xylophragma myrianthum
taxonomy: Xylophragma myrianthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. This family consists of around 2,964 species of flowering plants, mostly native to tropical and subtropical regions. As a member of this family, Xylophragma myrianthum is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The plant's classification also places it within the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Xylophragma myrianthum's classification helps scientists and botanists understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Xylophragma myrianthum, also known as the "Guatemalan coffee bush," is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
